summary: Phase-6 (Canvas UX & ops polish) tested end-to-end. Backend: 11/11 pytest passing (7 from /app/backend/tests/test_phase6.py + 4 new in /app/backend/tests/test_phase6_extended.py covering history-stack growth, analytics 10-key coverage, text-label PATCH persistence, conflicts grouping shape). Frontend: skip/dev login works, dashboard global undo/redo arrows visible, Analytics tab loads with all 10 cards populated from /api/analytics/summary, canvas opens with full Phase-6 toolbar (canvas-undo/redo/delete/toggle-guests/print) and palette tiles palette-line + palette-text. Guest side panel toggles open with search box (canvas-guest-search) and family cards (guest-card-{id}). canvas-delete is correctly disabled with no selection. Clicking palette-text drops a label object at canvas center as fallback (objects count went 11→12). Found one real bug: over_capacity conflict detection is broken for grouped/combined tables, causing /api/seating/conflicts to return 0 conflicts even though Table 1 (party-sum 14) + Table 2 (10) = 24 in a group of capacity 20.

critical.issue: over_capacity detection bug for grouped/combined tables. Loop in list_conflicts() (server.py ~line 1900) compares PER-TABLE seated count against GROUP capacity instead of summing all group members first. Live data: Tables 1+2 (group cc07a5ca, combined cap 20) hold 14+10=24 party-seats (4 over); Tables 3+1908 (group 3fb4983a, cap 20) hold 12+10=22 party-seats (2 over). Neither reported. Result: analytics-conflicts card and canvas red-dot badges always show 0 for combined tables.

minor.issue: Returns key 'partialSubmissions' but spec/wording in review_request mentions 'partiallySeatedSubmissions'. Frontend reads 'partialSubmissions' so wiring is fine. Naming nit only.

ui_bugs.issue: Pre-existing React hydration warning: '<span> cannot be a child of <option>'. Caused by Emergent visual-editor instrumentation; functionally harmless. Documented in iteration_5 — still present.

integration_issues.issue: Because backend conflicts endpoint misreports over_capacity for grouped tables, the red-dot badge (data-testid='conflict-badge-{tableId}') never renders for combined tables. Single (non-grouped) over-capacity tables would still surface correctly.

action_items: FIX backend /api/seating/conflicts over_capacity loop to group-aware aggregation: sum party_size across all tables sharing a group_id, then compare once per group against group_cap. Solo (group_id=NULL) tables continue using per-table cap. After fix, /api/analytics/summary->activeConflicts and canvas red-dot badges will populate correctly.

(Optional, cosmetic) Suppress or wrap <option>{tableNumber}</option> rendering so the visual-editor instrumentation does not inject a <span> inside <option> — eliminates the hydration warning on Guest List.
(Optional, naming) Decide whether to rename analytics key 'partialSubmissions' → 'partiallySeatedSubmissions' to match phase-6 spec wording (frontend would need same rename).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py L1898-1909: over_capacity check uses group_cap for eff but iterates seated_per_t per single tableId, so seated never aggregates to group level — guarantees false negatives whenever any table belongs to a group. Should pre-aggregate seated_per_group, then iterate groups once and per-non-grouped tables once.

server.py L1957-1961: when building byTableId for family_split conflicts, only the first tableId in c['tableIds'] is recorded; other affected tables won't show the badge. Consider attaching the family_split entry to ALL of c['tableIds'].

BallroomCanvas.jsx L1664: family-card testid is `guest-card-{id}` (not `canvas-guest-card-*` mentioned in some Phase-6 notes). Confirm naming convention with PM if external automation depends on the prefix.

success_rate.frontend: 100% of Phase-6 surface verified; 1 backend-rooted integration gap (conflict badges for combined tables)

seed_data_creation: Uses live Supabase data from iteration_5 (TEST_BigA/B/Smith/Jones/Excel guests, 4 tables across 2 groups). New tests create+delete one text canvas-object and one line canvas-object per run (TEST_TXT_<RUN_ID>) — fully cleaned up via DELETE in fixture teardown.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase-6 backend endpoints (history/undo, history/redo, history/stack, seating/conflicts, analytics/summary, PATCH canvas-objects, PATCH guests with invoiceNumber+seatingPreferences) all functional. UI surfaces are wired: dashboard header has global-undo/global-redo arrow buttons; canvas-toolbar has canvas-undo/redo/delete/toggle-guests/print; palette has palette-line + palette-text; analytics-tab has all 10 cards. Open canvas via 'Open canvas' button under Tables & Seating tab (it launches the full BallroomCanvas overlay). One real bug to verify after fix: combined-table over_capacity conflict detection — once fixed, /api/seating/conflicts should return at least 2 conflicts in current live DB (tables 1+2 and 3+1908). Hydration <option><span> warning is Emergent visual-editor injection, not app code.
rca of the issue: Conflict endpoint bug: in list_conflicts (server.py ~L1900) the iteration is `for tid, seated in seated_per_t.items()` comparing each table's per-table seated count against `group_cap.get(t['group_id'])`. When two tables share a group, both tables compare their individual seated total (e.g. 14, 10) against the COMBINED group cap (20), which trivially passes. Repro: live data shows tables 1+2 hold sum=24 in a 20-seat group yet endpoint returns conflicts=[]. Mitigation: aggregate seated per group_id first, then check group_total > group_cap once; for solo tables fall back to existing per-table compare.
